description: Computer engineering student at Purdue building embedded, verification, and performance-focused systems across hardware and software.

hero.name: Aditya Hebbani
hero.title: Computer Engineering
hero.p1: building embedded systems, verification infrastructure, and performance-focused tools—often at the boundary between hardware and software.
hero.p2: Experience across industrial automation, STM32 telemetry, chip verification (UVM/constrained-random), and Linux services deployed at scale.
hero.p3: Research spanning GPU microarchitecture/simulation and robotics perception, with a focus on correctness, observability, and practical deployability.

items.name: RISC-V CPU Design
items.description: Dual-core CPU pipeline work with cache coherence; exploring out-of-order directions (Tomasulo + ROB) and stress testing for bounded-latency behavior.

items.name: Maverick Micro Aerial Vehicles
items.description: Co-founded a micro aerial vehicles effort; led autonomy/vision, UART drivers, sensor PCB, and a low-latency UDP/GStreamer pipeline on Jetson Nano.

items.name: Purdue Aerial Robotics Team
items.description: Guidance & navigation leadership; designed avionics PCBs in Altium and built ROS-based vision + planning pipelines with multithreaded nodes for stable frame rate.

items.name: Ultrasound Segmentation (Transformer–UNet)
items.description: Led ultrasound segmentation research: built a Transformer–UNet model, curated 2k+ annotated images, and integrated outputs into a ROS-based robot workflow.

items.name: GPU Simulation & Micro-benchmarks
items.description: Built a Python GPU emulator modeling SIMT, memory hierarchies, and warp scheduling; wrote CUDA/PTX tests and micro-benchmarks to evaluate coalescing/divergence trade-offs.

items.name: Embedded + Industrial Tooling
items.description: STM32 telemetry platform work, PLC ladder logic, and Linux/Flask services integrating TCP sockets and Ethernet/IP devices for production reliability.

Built STM32-based sensor telemetry hardware/software, implemented PLC ladder logic and vision QC tooling, and deployed Linux/Flask services across 12 mills integrating TCP sockets and Ethernet/IP devices.
Verified a quad-core 130 nm CPU; built UVM constrained-random environments for core/cache subsystems; applied ATPG and wafer-level testing concepts to strengthen pre-production validation.
Led a team of interns building predictive demand models and interactive analytics tools, focusing on end-to-end workflows and disciplined evaluation.
Supported instruction and labs for Computer Prototyping; guided students through processor design and debugging workflows in QuestaSim.

Contributed to GPU backend components and evaluation via CUDA/PTX tests and micro-benchmarks; collaborated on compiler optimizations; built a Python GPU emulator for SIMT and memory/warp scheduling trade-off analysis.
Led segmentation research with a Transformer–UNet model, curated 2k+ annotated images, and integrated ROS workflows on a Sawyer robot for anatomy-guided probe control.

items.title: Embedded Systems
items.description: STM32 bring-up, sensor integration, interrupt-safe drivers, buffered telemetry, and reliability-focused debugging.

items.title: Digital Design / RTL
items.description: SystemVerilog/VHDL/Chisel, microarchitecture, and practical verification-aware design.

items.title: Verification (UVM)
items.description: Constrained-random methodology, testbench infrastructure, regressions, and debug-oriented workflows.

items.title: Computer Architecture
items.description: CPU pipeline design, caches/coherence, performance trade-offs, and test strategies for correctness under stress.

items.title: Linux + Tooling
items.description: Services, profiling/debugging (gdb/perf/valgrind/sanitizers), sockets, and IPC patterns.

items.title: ML / Perception
items.description: PyTorch/TensorFlow workflows, segmentation modeling, evaluation discipline, and integration into real systems.

contact.status: Open to internships and research opportunities
